overview: Oops! All Spells is a Legacy combo deck that mills its entire deck on turn 1 without playing any lands. It generates mana using Spirit Guides, Rituals, Lotus Petal, and MDFCs, then triggers Balustrade Spy or Undercity Informer to mill the deck. The resulting graveyard state enables Narcomoebas and Bridge from Below to fuel Cabal Therapy, ultimately resolving Dread Return's flashback to cast Thassa's Oracle and win the game.
win_conditions: Mill the entire deck using Balustrade Spy or Undercity Informer when zero lands are on the battlefield
Sacrifice Narcomoebas and Bridge from Below tokens to cast Cabal Therapy and find combo pieces
Pay the flashback cost of Dread Return to cast Thassa's Oracle and win immediately

keep_criteria: Hands that can execute the full combo on turn 1
Hands containing the combo, protection, and sufficient mana
Hands that can set up a turn 2 combo under Pact of Negation protection (only in rare, known matchups)

Keep hands with 2+ mana sources (including cantrips/artifacts like Lotus Petal, Mishra's Bauble, or Mox Opal) and at least one key piece (Agadeem's Awakening, Karn, Cauldron Familiar, or Balustrade Spy).
mulligan_criteria: Hands that cannot combo on turn 1
Hands lacking protection against Thoughtseize but still attempting to combo (highly risky)
Hands that do not combo on turn 1 and are not cutting to 4 cards
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no spells, or hands with only one mana source and no draw/disruption spells.

examples.reason: You just need to draw 1 black mana to combo on turn 2 with this hand, all under Pact's protection. It's worth the risk, right? Remember what I said above? If you can't combo on turn 1, you need to send the whole hand back.

This hand doesn't combo on turn 1, so: Mulligan. However, it is an exception if you know your opponent's deck because you can play Undercity Informer on turn 1 with it and have enough mana to activate it on turn 2.
This hand has the combo, but doesn't have the mana to protect it against Thoughtseize . Do you risk it? Of course, you've already committed to going all-in when you decided to play this deck!
This hand has the combo, protection, and mana. It is the reason this deck sees play. Verdict: Instant keep.

early_game: Mulligan aggressively until a turn 1 combo hand is found
Generate exactly 4 mana (including 1 black) using Spirit Guides, Rituals, Lotus Petal, and MDFCs
Play Balustrade Spy or activate Undercity Informer to mill the entire deck
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Lotus Petal/Mishra's Bauble for mana, or cast Thoughtseize/Inquisition to disrupt.
Turn 2: Cast Mox Opal/Balustrade Spy, draw cards, and start assembling the spell engine.
Sequence lands carefully to ensure you can cast multiple instant-speed spells by turn 3-4.
mid_game: Sacrifice Narcomoebas and Bridge from Below tokens to cast Cabal Therapy
Use Cabal Therapy to locate Dread Return and Thassa's Oracle
Cast Dread Return for flashback to return Thassa's Oracle from the graveyard
Prioritize keeping cheap artifacts and discard in hand for protection.
Use Cauldron Familiar to flashback spells from the graveyard to fuel Balustrade Spy and Agadeem's Awakening.
Don't overcommit to the graveyard if facing Rest in Peace
Hold back on casting Agadeem's Awakening until you have enough cheap spells in hand/graveyard to draw cards and generate tokens safely.
Use discard and spot interaction to clear threats before going off.
late_game: Win immediately when Thassa's Oracle enters the battlefield
Use Memory's Journey flashback to reshuffle the graveyard and attempt a second combo if disrupted
Use Jack-o'-Lantern to filter mana for flashback costs or Oracle's second blue mana
Agadeem's Awakening is the primary finisher, drawing cards and creating Saproling tokens to win via combat or overwhelming board state.
Karn, the Great Creator can also close games by making artifacts cost less or tutoring for pieces.
Go all-in when you have 3+ mana sources, at least one cheap artifact or discard, and a clear path to casting Agadeem's Awakening without being countered or disrupted.

key_cards.name: Balustrade Spy
key_cards.notes: Triggers mill effect when deck has no lands

key_cards.name: Undercity Informer
key_cards.notes: Activated ability mills deck if no lands are on battlefield

key_cards.name: Thassa's Oracle
key_cards.notes: Wins game immediately upon entering battlefield if deck has no cards

key_cards.name: Dread Return
key_cards.notes: Flashback returns Oracle from graveyard to battlefield

key_cards.name: Narcomoeba
key_cards.notes: Enters battlefield when milled, sacrificed for Cabal Therapy

key_cards.name: Bridge from Below
key_cards.notes: Creates tokens to sacrifice for additional Cabal Therapies

key_cards.name: Cabal Therapy
key_cards.notes: Finds combo pieces and removes opponent's threats

key_cards.name: Pact of Negation
key_cards.notes: Counters spells to protect the combo from interaction

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Removes opponent's key cards before or during combo

key_cards.name: Memory's Journey
key_cards.notes: Flashback reshuffles graveyard to combo again if disrupted

key_cards.name: Jack-o'-Lantern
key_cards.notes: Provides black/white mana for flashback or Oracle's cost

key_cards.name: Simian Spirit Guide
key_cards.notes: Tapped for 1 mana, counts as a spell for mill triggers

key_cards.name: Elvish Spirit Guide
key_cards.notes: Tapped for 1 mana, counts as a spell for mill triggers

key_cards.name: Lotus Petal
key_cards.notes: Tapped for 1 colorless mana, counts as a spell

key_cards.name: Dark Ritual
key_cards.notes: Provides instant black mana for combo

key_cards.name: Fell the Profane
key_cards.notes: MDFC front side counts as spell, back side is land

sequencing_tips: Generate exactly 4 mana (including 1 black) before activating the mill trigger
Play Pact of Negation or Thoughtseize before or during the combo to bypass opponent's interaction
Sacrifice Narcomoebas and Bridge from Below tokens in sequence to cast multiple Cabal Therapies
Use Jack-o'-Lantern's ability to filter mana for Memory's Journey flashback or Thassa's Oracle's second blue mana
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the graveyard against Rest in Peace.
Always have a backup plan with hand disruption.
Balustrade Spy is crucial for card advantage, so protect it.
Agadeem's Awakening wins via combat if you have enough Saprolings, so don't forget to attack.
Watch for Leyline of the Void in opening hand
if opponent has it, consider mulliganing or playing around it with discard.

matchups.advice: Overwhelm defenses with combo hands and protection; add more disruption post-side.
Play around Chalice/Vexing Bauble by using Memory's Journey to return Reanimate; ignore Leyline by exiling deck and playing Oracle from hand.
Perform well in game 1; sideboard for graveyard hate.
Open turn 1 by discarding to understand threats, then combo on turn 2; apply same strategy post-side.
Vulnerable in game 1; stacking Oracles is weak due to Pyroblast.

Use multiple cheap spells to out-value counters, cast Agadeem's Awakening as the last spell in a chain, or use discard to remove counterspells from hand. Flusterstorm can also protect your key spells.
Apply early pressure with discard and cheap artifacts. Use Tormod's Crypt or Surgical Extraction to remove their mana rocks. Combo off quickly before they can stabilize.
Use Thoughtseize/Inquisition to remove key threats, Flusterstorm/Spell Pierce to protect your combo, and Tormod's Crypt for graveyard decks. Stabilize and go off on turn 3-4.

Win the interaction war with Flusterstorm, Spell Pierce, and Thoughtseize. Prioritize graveyard hate (Tormod's Crypt) to disrupt their recursion. Out-value them with better sequencing and card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Flusterstorm, Spell Pierce, Force of Will), graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, Tormod's Crypt), and board wipes.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: create 4 mana (1 should be black), and then either play Balustrade Spy or play and activate Undercity Informer . The result in either case is the same: your deck has no lands, and, therefore, you'll mill it all at once.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Once you activate their abilities, your entire deck will go to your graveyard and, thus, Narcomoeba s will enter play. Then, you can sacrifice them for Cabal Therapy to make sure nothing will stand in your way. Bridge From Below and Poxwalkers will give you all the extra bodies you need, and, finally, you'll pay for Dread Return 's Flashback cost, and Thassa's Oracle will enter play to finish the game.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: create 4 mana (1 should be black), and then either play Balustrade Spy or play and activate Undercity Informer .
If Jack-o'-Lantern is in your graveyard, you'll be able to filter mana and either pay for Memory's Journey 's Flashback or Thassa's Oracle second blue mana
Then, you can sacrifice them for Cabal Therapy to make sure nothing will stand in your way.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Thoughtseize and Pact of Negation will help you play your combo through enemy defenses.
usually as an answer to a Faerie Macabre or a Surgical Extraction .
At most, they can play Chalice of the Void or Vexing Bauble , which disrupt but don't interrupt your combo completely.
Their main graveyard answer is Leyline of the Void .
Stacking your Thassa's Oracle s is also not that strong in this case because they play several Pyroblast s.
